close_reason: Accepted SDK limitation. bitwarden-sm's error types are private and use #[error(transparent)]. String-matching Display output is the only option. Already well-documented with upgrade guidance.
description: In secretx-bitwarden/src/lib.rs, classify_bitwarden_sdk_error determines whether an error is transient (Unavailable) or permanent (Backend) by matching substrings in the Display output of bitwarden_sdk errors — patterns like '[5', '[429]', 'error sending request', and 'timed out'. This is fragile: if the bitwarden-core crate changes its error message format in any minor version, the classification silently breaks. A 5xx error that no longer starts with '[5' would become Backend (non-retriable) instead of Unavailable, causing callers with retry logic to stop retrying on transient server errors. The SDK error type should be matched structurally (on enum variants or error codes) rather than by parsing the Display string.
title: bitwarden: error classification by Display string matching
